what_it_does: Acceptance test suite that exercises the conversational `request_change` RHM verb routing a chat change request into the existing `surface_intent_at` producer so it mints an `intent==build` inbox item surfaced for operator approval through the same resolve path used by the wire-door, and verifies deterministically (temp store, no claude -p, no arming) that resolvable targets mint build intents, unresolvable targets ask instead, the verb derives from the single VerbSpec, and existing wire/propose/panel/extend paths are unchanged.
observations: Uses a temporary FsStore to stay deterministic and avoid the live store.
Tests are executed directly under `__main__` and tally a global `PASS` counter.
Verifies the native tool is built from the spec and `_json_obj_to_action` normalizes the tool-call shape.
Tests address resolution tiers: indicated locus, named element, and ask.
Tests precedence: an explicit resolvable target wins over a stale session locus; a usable locus wins over an unresolvable name.
Tests unresolvable cases (no target, unknown name, unregistered `ui://`, empty change) result in `ask_target` or `none` and no inbox growth.
Tests the wire-door `surface_build_intent` and `surface_intent_at` paths remain unchanged.
Monkey-patches `runtime.implement.launch` to prove the default wire posture is built-not-armed.
Tests `propose` drafts are not build intents and forbidden verbs are still refused.
standouts: The explicit-target-beats-stale-locus test is flagged as correctness-critical to prevent wrong-scope builds.
The built-not-armed test monkey-patches `implement.launch` to prove the default posture prevents execution.
The spec-derivation test enforces single-source VerbSpec projection for the new verb.
conventions: Heavy docstrings on the module and each test function explaining intent and rules.
Global `PASS` counter with a `check` helper that asserts and reports.
Relative `sys.path` insertion to locate the package.
Temporary directory for an isolated store fixture.
Monkey-patching wrapped in `try/finally` with restoration and environment cleanup.
Direct use of internal Suite methods and attributes for dispatch and state inspection.
concerns: Tests depend on internal/private Suite methods and attributes (e.g., `_dispatch_rhm_action`, `_current_locus`, `_VERB_PARAMS`, `_rhm_tools`, `resolve_change_target`), making them brittle to refactors.
Hard-coded element addresses and names (e.g., `ui://toolbar/run`, `ui://chrome/activity`, `the run button`, `the inbox`) assume a specific registry corpus.
Global `PASS` counter and side-effect assertions inside the `check` helper.
`pick_registered_element` fallback returns `ui://chrome/inbox`, which the comment notes is a region served-form rather than a guaranteed element row.
Only `test_reaches_wire_trigger_built_not_armed` restores environment and launch patch; other tests do not mutate them.
notes: A deterministic acceptance test that closes the chat-to-build-intent loop by proving `request_change` mints the same approval-gated build intent as the wire-door while refusing to guess scope.
questions: What is the exact shape of the VerbSpec and the `RHM_VERBS`, `RHM_VERB_CLASS`, and `_VERB_PARAMS` data structures in `runtime/suite.py`?
How does `resolve_change_target` perform name matching, ambiguity detection, and `ui://` validation?
What registry corpus is loaded from `nodes/` and which addresses/names are guaranteed to exist?
What is the intended behavior when both a resolvable target and a current locus are present, and is explicit-target precedence always correct?
How does `surface_intent_at` differ from `surface_build_intent`, and why does the conversational verb route through the former?
What are the exact semantics of `is_build_intent` and the `decision.intent` event?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose—deterministically prove the conversational `request_change` verb routes into the existing build-intent surface and approval flow—matches the implemented tests, including the safety and regression coverage described.
apparent_themes: No guessed scope / fail loud on ambiguity
intention_signals: The file is trying to validate that chat-driven change requests are first-class build intents, indistinguishable from wire-door intents in approval and execution gating, while preserving all existing paths and refusing to silently fabricate targets.
novelty: It treats a new conversational verb as a routing layer into an existing build-intent producer rather than a separate code path, and tests that equivalence through the same inbox/resolve events.
contribution: A deterministic, self-contained acceptance harness that documents and guards the chat-to-build-intent bridge, the address-resolution precedence rules, the built-not-armed default, and the regression boundary with propose/panel/extend and the wire-door.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for the conversational `request_change` RHM verb verifying it mints approval-gated `intent==build` inbox items via the existing `surface_intent_at` producer, resolves addresses without guessing, derives from the single VerbSpec, and leaves wire-door/propose paths unchanged.
intention_for_embedding: To prove deterministically that chat-initiated change requests surface through the same operator-approved build-intent lifecycle as wire-door intents while staying safe-by-default.

symbols.does: Increments a global pass counter after asserting a labeled condition.
symbols.description: Helper that asserts `cond` with a labeled failure message, increments the module-level `PASS` counter on success, and prints an ok line; used by all tests to report individual checks.

symbols.does: Creates an isolated Suite instance backed by a temporary FsStore and discovered nodes.
symbols.description: Builds a fresh `Suite` with a temporary `FsStore`, discovers nodes from the `nodes/` directory, and returns it; provides deterministic, isolated test fixtures.

symbols.does: Returns a real registered `ui://` element address for scope derivation.
symbols.description: Scans the UI info corpus for a string key starting with `ui://` and returns it as a registered element address; falls back to `ui://chrome/inbox` so tests have a resolvable target.

symbols.does: Lists ids of unresolved inbox items.
symbols.description: Returns the `id` values of all inbox entries whose `resolved` field is `None`; used to verify that build intents surface as open review items and that asks do not grow the inbox.

symbols.does: Verifies `request_change` is projected from the single VerbSpec and offered in build modes.
symbols.description: Checks that `request_change` appears in `RHM_VERBS`, `RHM_VERB_DESC`, `RHM_VERB_CLASS`, and `_VERB_PARAMS`; that it is offered in build-ish modes but not observe/off modes; that the native tool is built from the spec; and that `_json_obj_to_action` normalizes its call shape.

symbols.does: Proves a resolvable address mints an approval-gated build intent.
symbols.description: Dispatches `request_change` against a registered element, asserts the surfaced item has `payload.intent == 'build'`, `is_build_intent` is true, `resolved` is None, `action` is review, the inbox grows by one, and a `decision.intent` event is emitted.

symbols.does: Proves a missing target falls back to the operator's current locus.
symbols.description: Sets `suite._current_locus` to a registered address, dispatches `request_change` with no target, and verifies the build intent is scoped to the indicated address with `source == 'indicated'`.

symbols.does: Verifies an explicit resolvable target wins over a stale session locus.
symbols.description: Tests `resolve_change_target` and dispatch precedence: a named resolvable target is chosen over a stale `current_locus`, no target still uses the locus, and a usable locus is preferred over an unresolvable name.

symbols.does: Verifies natural-language element names resolve to `ui://` addresses.
symbols.description: Resolves `the run button` to `ui://toolbar/run`, dispatches `request_change` with that name, and confirms a build intent is minted; also checks that an ambiguous name like `the inbox` asks instead of guessing.

symbols.does: Proves unresolvable targets ask and never mint guessed build intents.
symbols.description: Dispatches `request_change` with no target, an unknown name, an unregistered `ui://` address, and an empty change description; asserts each results in `ask_target` or `none` and the open inbox count never increases.

symbols.does: Confirms the existing wire-door and intent-at paths still mint build intents.
symbols.description: Calls `surface_build_intent` and `surface_intent_at`, verifies both produce `is_build_intent` items with `resolved=None`, and checks that a plain review item is not misclassified as a build intent.

symbols.does: Proves approval routes through the same trigger path but stays safe by default.
symbols.description: Mints a conversational build intent, approves it via `resolve_surfaced`, monkey-patches `implement.launch`, and asserts that with the wire unarmed no launch occurs, no `decision.dispatch` event is emitted, and status is not `implemented`.

symbols.does: Confirms Channel A verbs remain non-build drafts and forbidden verbs are refused.
symbols.description: Dispatches `propose` and verifies any surfaced item is not a build intent, then dispatches a forbidden verb and confirms it is refused, guarding the RHM whitelist boundary.
